# dotuix

> dotuix is an open-source ecosystem for the `.uix` executable document format.
> A `.uix` file is an interactive, offline-first, tamper-evident application
> distributed as a single portable file — like PDF, but it runs.

## What is .uix?

`.uix` is the **executable document format**. It is a standard ZIP archive
containing a self-contained HTML/JS/CSS application, optional assets, and
optional SQLite databases. The viewer opens it fully offline with no install,
no URL, and no server.

Mental model:
- PDF = printable document
- EPUB = readable document
- `.uix` = **executable document**

Use cases: classified government briefings, legal reference systems, offline
healthcare databases, audit reports, education simulations, restaurant kiosks,
sales proposals — any interactive experience that must be self-contained,
offline, and distributable as a single file.

---

## Archive structure

```
myapp.uix  (ZIP — standard ZIP format)
├── manifest.json       ← REQUIRED. Describes the app.
├── index.html          ← Entry point declared in manifest.entry
├── app.js
├── style.css
├── assets/             ← Convention: images, video, audio, fonts
├── files/              ← Convention: PDFs, CSVs, JSON data
├── data.db             ← Optional: read-only SQLite (creator data)
└── state.db            ← Optional: read-write SQLite (user state / seed)
```

Compression rules:
- HTML, CSS, JS, JSON → DEFLATE
- PNG, JPG, WEBP, GIF, data.db, state.db → STORE (already compressed or binary)

`assets/` and `files/` are conventions, not enforced. Files can live anywhere
and are referenced by relative path from the entry HTML.

---

## manifest.json — complete field reference

```json
{
  "uix": "1.0",
  "id": "com.almadina.menu",
  "name": "Al Madina Restaurant Menu",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "minViewer": "1.0.0",
  "entry": "index.html",
  "mode": "kiosk",
  "permissions": ["local-storage", "print"],
  "network": "blocked",
  "theme": {
    "color": "#1a1a2e",
    "background": "#ffffff"
  },
  "author": "author@example.com",
  "expires": null,
  "state": {
    "seed": false
  }
}
```

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `uix` | string | Yes | Format version. Always `"1.0"` for current format. |
| `id` | string | Yes | Stable reverse-domain id. e.g. `"com.almadina.menu"`. No DNS enforcement — convention only. Used for state isolation, caching, and signatures. |
| `name` | string | Yes | Human-readable app name shown in viewer chrome. |
| `version` | string | Yes | SemVer app version. e.g. `"1.0.0"`. |
| `minViewer` | string | No | Minimum viewer version required. Viewer refuses with a clear message if below. |
| `entry` | string | Yes | Path to entry HTML file inside archive. e.g. `"index.html"`. |
| `mode` | string | Yes | `"kiosk"` — locked UI, no address bar, no right-click. `"window"` — developer preview with toolbar. |
| `permissions` | string[] | No | Capabilities the app is allowed to use (see list below). |
| `network` | string | No | `"blocked"` (default) or `"allowed"`. Blocked = viewer's CSP blocks all external requests. |
| `theme` | object | No | Viewer chrome colors: `color` (hex) and `background` (hex). |
| `author` | string | No | Creator email or identifier. |
| `expires` | string\|null | No | ISO 8601 date string or `null`. Viewer checks expiry **before extraction** — expired files never unpack. |
| `state.seed` | boolean | No | `true` = copy `state.db` from archive as starting user state on first open. Default `false`. |
| `security` | object | No | Optional PIN auth + encryption. Omit entirely for regular apps. |
| `signature` | object | No | Optional Ed25519 signature. Written by `dotuix sign`. |
| `ai` | object | No | Optional AI provenance block. Omit for non-AI-generated files. |

### Allowed permissions values

- `"local-storage"` — browser localStorage access
- `"print"` — system print dialog
- `"clipboard-write"` — write to clipboard
- `"fullscreen"` — request fullscreen
- `"raw-sql"` — enable `uix.data.raw()` and `uix.state.raw()` for arbitrary SQL queries

### Security block (optional — omit entirely for regular apps)

```json
{
  "security": {
    "auth": "pin",
    "encryptedPaths": ["data.db", "files/annex.pdf"],
    "kdf": "PBKDF2-SHA256",
    "kdfIterations": 200000,
    "keySalt": "base64url-random-salt-here",
    "maxOpens": 3,
    "screenshot": false
  },
  "signature": {
    "algorithm": "Ed25519",
    "publicKey": "base64url-public-key",
    "value": "base64url-signature",
    "signedAt": "2026-05-19T10:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

| security field | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `auth` | `"none"` | `"pin"` = viewer prompts for PIN before opening. |
| `encryptedPaths` | `[]` | Paths inside archive encrypted with AES-256-GCM. Decrypted in memory after auth. |
| `kdf` | `"PBKDF2-SHA256"` | Key derivation function. |
| `kdfIterations` | 200000 | PBKDF2 iteration count. |
| `keySalt` | — | Base64url random salt (stored in manifest, safe to store publicly). |
| `maxOpens` | unlimited | Max times this file may be opened. Tracked by viewer locally. |
| `screenshot` | `false` | `true` = viewer blocks OS screenshot while file is open (desktop only). |

### AI provenance block (optional)

When a `.uix` file is generated by an AI model or tool, include the `ai` block
in `manifest.json` to declare provenance. This block is informational and has
no effect on the viewer — it does not gate loading or change behaviour.

```json
{
  "ai": {
    "generatedBy": "claude-opus-4",
    "generatedAt": "2026-05-19T12:00:00Z",
    "capabilities": ["search", "chat"],
    "promptHash": "sha256-hex-of-prompt"
  }
}
```

| ai field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `generatedBy` | string | Model or tool that created this file, e.g. `"claude-opus-4"`, `"@dotuix/ai"`, `"@dotuix/mcp"`. |
| `generatedAt` | string | ISO-8601 timestamp of generation. |
| `capabilities` | string[] | Semantic capabilities this app exposes, e.g. `["search", "chat", "form"]`. |
| `promptHash` | string | SHA-256 hex digest of the generation prompt (for reproducibility). |

For AI agent apps (chat, workflows, tools), prefer `mode: "window"` over
`mode: "kiosk"` — it gives users the toolbar and navigation controls expected
in agent-driven interfaces.

---

## Data architecture

Always follow this split — it is the core pattern of every .uix app:

| What | Where | How |
|---|---|---|
| Content (menu items, products, articles, catalog) | `data.db` | creator populates before packing; app reads via `uix.data.find()` |
| User state (cart, settings, session, orders) | `state.db` | app reads/writes via `uix.state.*` at runtime |
| UI logic, rendering, event handlers | `app.js` | plain JavaScript — no data hardcoded here |

**Do not hardcode content in `app.js`.** If you have menu items, products, or
any creator-defined data, put them in `data.db` as records and load them with
`uix.data.find({ type: "product" })`. Hardcoded JS arrays cannot be updated
without repacking the entire file.

---

## The window.__uix bridge API

The viewer injects `window.__uix` (also aliased as `window.uix`) into the
running app. All methods return Promises. The app has no access to the host
filesystem — only through this bridge.

### App metadata

```javascript
const manifest = await uix.manifest();
// Returns the full manifest.json as a plain object
```

### Data database — read-only (creator data shipped in the .uix)

> **These are the only three methods on `uix.data`:** `find()`, `get()`, `raw()`.
> There is no `getAll`, `findAll`, `fetchAll`, `getItems`, `query`, `list`, `all`, or any other
> variant. If you need all records of a type, call `find({ type: "..." })` — it returns all of them.

```javascript
// Returns ALL records of a type — no extra argument needed
const products = await uix.data.find({ type: "product" });

// Filtered, sorted, paginated
const burgers = await uix.data.find({
  type: "product",
  where: { category: "burgers" },   // json_extract(body, '$.category') = 'burgers'
  orderBy: { field: "sort", direction: "asc" },  // or orderBy: "sort" (string shorthand)
  limit: 20,
});

// Get a single record by id
const product = await uix.data.get("product:001");

// Raw SQL — requires "raw-sql" in manifest permissions
const results = await uix.data.raw(
  "SELECT body FROM records WHERE type = ? ORDER BY json_extract(body, '$.sort') ASC",
  ["product"]
);
```

### State database — read-write (user data, persisted across opens)

```javascript
// Insert a new record
const record = await uix.state.insert({
  type: "cart_item",
  body: { productId: "product:001", qty: 2, price: 35.0 },
});
// Returns full record: { id, type, body, created_at, updated_at }
// id is auto-generated as "cart_item:<uuid>"

// Find records
const cartItems = await uix.state.find({ type: "cart_item" });

// Get single record by id
const item = await uix.state.get("cart_item:uuid");

// Update — second argument is the new body object (replaces the existing body)
await uix.state.update("cart_item:uuid", { qty: 3, price: 35.0 });

// Delete
await uix.state.delete("cart_item:uuid");

// Purge records older than a duration (units: s, m, h, d, y)
await uix.state.purge({ type: "session_log", olderThan: "24h" });

// Raw SQL — requires "raw-sql" permission
await uix.state.raw("DELETE FROM records WHERE type = ?", ["session_log"]);
```

### Record shape (both databases)

```typescript
interface Record {
  id: string;          // e.g. "product:001", "cart_item:a1b2c3"
  type: string;        // e.g. "product", "cart_item"
  body: string;        // JSON string — always JSON.parse(record.body) to read fields
  created_at: number;  // Unix timestamp — set on insert, never changed
  updated_at: number;  // Unix timestamp — updated on every update call
}
```

The `body` field is stored as a JSON string in SQLite.
- **Writing** (`insert`, `update`): pass a plain object — the bridge JSON-stringifies it automatically.
- **Reading** (`find`, `get`): `body` comes back as a string — always call `JSON.parse(record.body)` before accessing fields.

### Utilities

```javascript
// Trigger the system print dialog
uix.print();

// Close the .uix file and return to viewer home screen
await uix.exit();
```

### What the bridge does NOT provide

These APIs do **not** exist — do not generate code that uses them:

**Data retrieval — `find()` is the only method:**
- `uix.data.getAll()` — use `uix.data.find({ type: "..." })`
- `uix.data.findAll()` — use `uix.data.find({ type: "..." })`
- `uix.data.fetchAll()` — use `uix.data.find({ type: "..." })`
- `uix.data.getItems()` — use `uix.data.find({ type: "..." })`
- `uix.data.query()` / `uix.data.list()` / `uix.data.all()` — none of these exist

**Other non-existent APIs:**
- `uix.storage.set / uix.storage.get` — use `uix.state.insert / uix.state.find` instead
- `uix.notify()` — use your own in-app UI (toast div, alert, banner)
- `uix.fetch()` — network is blocked by default (`"network": "blocked"`)
- `uix.version()` — not exposed
- `uix.fs` — no host filesystem access
- `uix.sendAction() / uix.dispatch() / uix.emit()` — no event or action bus exists
- `window.__uix.db` — does not exist; use `uix.data.find / uix.state.insert`

**Correct pattern for saving data (e.g. checkout / order submission):**
```javascript
// Save an order at checkout — use uix.state.insert, not sendAction
async function placeOrder(cartItems, total) {
  await uix.state.insert({
    type: "order",
    body: { items: cartItems, total, placedAt: new Date().toISOString() },
  });
  // then clear the in-memory cart and show confirmation in your own UI
}
```

**Correct pattern for restoring cart on load:**
```javascript
async function restoreCart() {
  const saved = await uix.state.find({ type: "cart_item" });
  // body is a JSON string — always parse it before reading fields
  return saved.map(r => JSON.parse(r.body));
}
```

---

## SQLite schema (identical in both databases)

```sql
CREATE TABLE records (
  id         TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
  type       TEXT    NOT NULL,
  body       TEXT    NOT NULL,
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (unixepoch()),
  updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (unixepoch())
);
CREATE INDEX idx_type       ON records (type);
CREATE INDEX idx_created_at ON records (created_at);

-- meta table (state.db only — created by viewer on first open)
CREATE TABLE meta (
  key   TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  value TEXT
);
```

---

## Example: Minimal working .uix project

To generate a valid `.uix`, create a folder with these files and run
`dotuix pack ./my-app`:

**manifest.json**
```json
{
  "uix": "1.0",
  "id": "com.example.myapp",
  "name": "My App",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "entry": "index.html",
  "mode": "kiosk",
  "network": "blocked"
}
```

**index.html**
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>My App</title>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">
</head>
<body>
  <h1>Hello from .uix</h1>
  <div id="app"></div>
  <script src="app.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
```

**app.js**
```javascript
(async () => {
  const manifest = await uix.manifest();
  document.querySelector('h1').textContent = manifest.name;

  // Load creator data — body is a JSON string, always JSON.parse before reading fields
  const products = await uix.data.find({ type: "product" });
  const app = document.getElementById('app');
  app.innerHTML = products.map(p => {
    const b = JSON.parse(p.body);
    return `<div class="product">${b.name} — ${b.price}</div>`;
  }).join('');
})();
```

---

## Example: Restaurant kiosk with SQLite

**manifest.json**
```json
{
  "uix": "1.0",
  "id": "com.almadina.menu",
  "name": "Al Madina Restaurant",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "entry": "index.html",
  "mode": "kiosk",
  "permissions": ["local-storage"],
  "network": "blocked"
}
```

**data.db** — SQLite with records table populated by creator:
```sql
-- Populate with the creator's menu items
INSERT INTO records (id, type, body) VALUES
  ('product:001', 'product', '{"name":"مشاوي مشكلة","nameEn":"Mixed Grill","price":85,"currency":"QAR","category":"grills"}'),
  ('product:002', 'product', '{"name":"حمص","nameEn":"Hummus","price":18,"currency":"QAR","category":"appetizers"}'),
  ('category:grills', 'category', '{"name":"مشويات","nameEn":"Grills","sort":1}');
```

**app.js**
```javascript
(async () => {
  // Load menu from data.db (read-only, creator data)
  const categories = await uix.data.find({ type: "category", orderBy: { field: "sort", direction: "asc" } });
  const products = await uix.data.find({ type: "product" });

  // Add to cart (persisted in state.db)
  async function addToCart(productId, qty, price) {
    const existing = await uix.state.find({ type: "cart_item", where: { productId } });
    if (existing.length > 0) {
      const b = JSON.parse(existing[0].body); // body is always a JSON string
      await uix.state.update(existing[0].id, { ...b, qty: b.qty + qty });
    } else {
      await uix.state.insert({ type: "cart_item", body: { productId, qty, price } });
    }
  }

  // Get cart total — parse body before reading fields
  const cartItems = await uix.state.find({ type: "cart_item" });
  const total = cartItems.reduce((sum, item) => {
    const b = JSON.parse(item.body);
    return sum + b.qty * b.price;
  }, 0);
})();
```

---

## CLI reference

```bash
# Install globally
npm install -g @dotuix/cli

# Create a new project
dotuix init my-app                    # blank starter
dotuix init my-app -t restaurant      # Gulf restaurant kiosk template
dotuix init my-app -t catalog         # product catalog template
dotuix init my-app -t portfolio       # portfolio/showcase template

# Pack a directory into .uix
dotuix pack ./my-app                  # → my-app.uix in current dir
dotuix pack ./my-app -o dist/         # → dist/my-app.uix

# Validate
dotuix validate my-app.uix

# Inspect
dotuix info my-app.uix                # prints manifest

# Sign (requires keygen first)
dotuix keygen my-key                  # generates my-key.private + my-key.public
dotuix sign my-app.uix --key my-key.private

# Encrypt selected paths
dotuix encrypt my-app.uix --paths data.db --pin-prompt

# Export state data
dotuix export my-app.uix --type order --format csv --output orders.csv
dotuix export my-app.uix --type order --format json --output orders.json
```

---

## Node.js / TypeScript API

```typescript
import { UIX } from "@dotuix/core";

// Pack a directory into .uix
await UIX.pack("./my-app", "./dist/my-app.uix");

// Unpack .uix to directory
await UIX.unpack("./my-app.uix", "./extracted/");

// Validate — returns { valid: boolean, errors: string[], warnings: string[] }
const result = await UIX.validate("./my-app.uix");

// Read manifest without unpacking
const manifest = await UIX.manifest("./my-app.uix");

// Open data.db for reading
const db = await UIX.openData("./my-app.uix");
```

---

## How to generate a .uix file with AI assistance

> **If you are an AI reading this spec:** Do NOT construct a ZIP file manually and do NOT hardcode
> content data in `app.js`. You cannot create a valid `data.db` SQLite file by hand.
> Always use `POST https://mcp.dotuix.uts.qa/api/create` — it builds the `.uix` for you
> and returns a download URL. See the REST API section below for the exact request format.

To generate a `.uix` file using any AI agent (GPT, Gemini, Claude, etc.):

1. Share this spec URL with your AI: `https://mcp.dotuix.uts.qa/api/spec`
2. Describe what you want: "Create a restaurant menu .uix for a Gulf Arabic restaurant with 10 items in QAR"
3. The AI calls `POST https://mcp.dotuix.uts.qa/api/create` with the full payload
4. The server returns a download URL — share it with the user

**The AI must never build the ZIP or SQLite file manually.** `data.db` is a binary SQLite file that
the server generates automatically from `dataRecords`. Any AI that constructs the archive itself will
produce a broken or non-compliant `.uix` (missing `data.db`, hardcoded JS arrays, wrong compression).

**For AI agents with tool use — remote MCP (no install):**
Any MCP-capable agent can connect to `https://mcp.dotuix.uts.qa/mcp` with just a URL.
No `npx`, no local packages. The remote server exposes `get_spec`, `create`, and `validate`.

Claude Desktop (`~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`):
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "dotuix": {
      "url": "https://mcp.dotuix.uts.qa/mcp"
    }
  }
}
```

Cursor / Windsurf (`.cursor/mcp.json` or `.windsurf/mcp.json` at workspace root):
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "dotuix": {
      "url": "https://mcp.dotuix.uts.qa/mcp"
    }
  }
}
```

OpenAI ChatGPT (GPT-4o with tool use via Actions / custom GPT):
Add a custom action pointing to `https://mcp.dotuix.uts.qa/mcp` — the server is
MCP-compliant (Streamable HTTP transport). Use the OpenAPI spec at
`https://mcp.dotuix.uts.qa/mcp/openapi.json` if the client requires it.

Gemini (Google AI Studio / Gemini API with function calling):
Pass `get_spec`, `create`, and `validate` as function declarations to
`tools[]` in your API call, with `https://mcp.dotuix.uts.qa/mcp` as the endpoint.

**For AI agents with tool use — local MCP (stdio):**
Install `@dotuix/mcp`. The agent calls `get_spec` first to read this spec, then
calls `create` to generate and pack a complete `.uix` in a single step.

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "dotuix": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@dotuix/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

Pass `dataRecords` to seed `data.db` — never hardcode content in `app.js`:

```json
{
  "tool": "create",
  "name": "my-restaurant",
  "manifest": { "uix": "1.0", "id": "com.almadina.menu", "name": "Al Madina", "version": "1.0.0", "entry": "index.html", "mode": "kiosk" },
  "files": [
    { "path": "index.html", "content": "<html>...</html>" },
    { "path": "app.js", "content": "(async () => { const products = await uix.data.find({ type: 'product' }); /* render */ })();" },
    { "path": "style.css", "content": "..." }
  ],
  "dataRecords": [
    { "id": "product:001", "type": "product", "body": { "name": "Mixed Grill", "price": 85, "currency": "QAR", "category": "grills" } },
    { "id": "product:002", "type": "product", "body": { "name": "Hummus",      "price": 18, "currency": "QAR", "category": "appetizers" } },
    { "id": "category:grills",      "type": "category", "body": { "name": "Grills",      "sort": 1 } },
    { "id": "category:appetizers",  "type": "category", "body": { "name": "Appetizers",  "sort": 2 } }
  ]
}
```

The MCP server creates `data.db`, seeds it with your records, and packs
everything. `ai.generatedBy` and `ai.generatedAt` are stamped automatically.
The returned `path` is the absolute path to the ready `.uix` file on disk.

**For AI-generated Node.js scripts:**
Use `@dotuix/ai` — a one-function SDK that handles temp dirs, manifest stamping,
and packing:

```typescript
import { createUIX } from "@dotuix/ai";

const path = await createUIX({
  manifest: {
    uix: "1.0",
    id: "com.example.myapp",
    name: "My App",
    version: "1.0.0",
    entry: "index.html",
    mode: "window",
  },
  files: {
    "index.html": "<html><body><h1>Hello</h1></body></html>",
    "app.js": "console.log('ready');",
  },
});
console.log(path); // absolute path to the packed .uix file
```

**For AI agents using HTTP REST (GPT, Gemini, custom agents — no MCP required):**

Call `POST https://mcp.dotuix.uts.qa/api/create` directly. Read the
spec first with `GET https://mcp.dotuix.uts.qa/api/spec`.

Request body (JSON):
```json
{
  "name": "AlMadinaKiosk",
  "manifest": {
    "uix": "1.0",
    "id": "com.almadina.kiosk",
    "name": "Al Madina Restaurant",
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "entry": "index.html",
    "mode": "kiosk",
    "network": "blocked"
  },
  "files": [
    { "path": "index.html", "content": "<!DOCTYPE html><html><head><link rel=\"stylesheet\" href=\"style.css\"></head><body><div id=\"app\"></div><script src=\"app.js\"></script></body></html>" },
    { "path": "app.js",     "content": "/* see correct app.js pattern below */" },
    { "path": "style.css",  "content": "body { font-family: sans-serif; }" }
  ],
  "dataRecords": [
    { "id": "category:grills",      "type": "category", "body": { "nameEn": "Grills",      "nameAr": "مشويات",   "sort": 1 } },
    { "id": "category:appetizers",  "type": "category", "body": { "nameEn": "Appetizers",  "nameAr": "مقبلات",   "sort": 2 } },
    { "id": "menuItem:grill-001",   "type": "menuItem", "body": { "nameEn": "Mixed Grill", "nameAr": "مشاوي مشكلة", "price": 85, "currency": "QAR", "category": "grills",      "emoji": "🍖" } },
    { "id": "menuItem:app-001",     "type": "menuItem", "body": { "nameEn": "Hummus",      "nameAr": "حمص",         "price": 18, "currency": "QAR", "category": "appetizers",  "emoji": "🥣" } },
    { "id": "i18n:en", "type": "i18n", "body": { "lang": "en", "restaurant": "Al Madina", "yourOrder": "Your Order", "total": "Total", "placeOrder": "Place Order", "emptyCart": "Cart is empty", "add": "Add", "langBtn": "العربية" } },
    { "id": "i18n:ar", "type": "i18n", "body": { "lang": "ar", "restaurant": "مطعم المدينة", "yourOrder": "طلبك", "total": "المجموع", "placeOrder": "تأكيد الطلب", "emptyCart": "السلة فارغة", "add": "أضف", "langBtn": "English" } }
  ]
}
```

Response:
```json
{ "downloadUrl": "https://mcp.dotuix.uts.qa/download/<id>", "filename": "AlMadinaKiosk.uix", "expiresIn": "30 minutes" }
```

**Critical rules for `POST /api/create`:**
- `files` is an array of `{ "path": string, "content": string }` — source files only
- **DO NOT put data inside `files`** (e.g. no `dataRecords.json` file) — data goes in `dataRecords`
- `dataRecords` is a separate top-level field — an array of `{ id, type, body }` records
- The server converts `dataRecords` → `data.db` (SQLite) automatically
- `body` in `dataRecords` is a **plain object** — the server stringifies it; do NOT pre-stringify
- `manifest.json` is NOT included in `files` — it is the separate `manifest` field

**Correct `app.js` pattern when using the REST API or MCP:**
```javascript
// uix is available as a global — no import or assignment needed
// uix === window.__uix === window.uix — use whichever; plain `uix` is preferred

async function init() {
  // 1. Load creator data from data.db (read-only)
  // find() returns an array of { id, type, body, created_at, updated_at }
  // body is ALWAYS a JSON string — you must JSON.parse() before reading fields
  const catRecords  = await uix.data.find({ type: "category" });
  const itemRecords = await uix.data.find({ type: "menuItem" });

  const categories = catRecords
    .map(r => JSON.parse(r.body))   // body is a JSON string — always parse
    .sort((a, b) => a.sort - b.sort);

  const menuItems = itemRecords.map(r => JSON.parse(r.body));

  // 2. Restore user state from state.db (REQUIRED on every startup)
  // uix.state is NOT auto-injected — you must read it back yourself.
  // If you write with uix.state.insert() but never call uix.state.find() here,
  // state will appear empty every time the app opens.
  const savedCart = await uix.state.find({ type: "cart_item" });
  const cart = savedCart.map(r => JSON.parse(r.body)); // body is a JSON string — always parse

  // 3. Render
  render(categories, menuItems, cart);
}

init().catch(err => {
  document.getElementById("app").innerHTML = `<p style="color:red">Error: ${err.message}</p>`;
});
```

**Common mistakes that cause a blank screen:**
- ❌ `uix.data.getAll()` / `findAll()` / `fetchAll()` — none of these exist; only `uix.data.find({ type: "..." })`
- ❌ `window.__uix.storage` — does not exist; use `uix.state.insert/find`
- ❌ `window.__uix.db` — does not exist; use `uix.data.find / uix.state.insert`
- ❌ Putting a `dataRecords.json` file in `files[]` — the viewer never reads it; use `dataRecords` field
- ❌ Reading `r.body.someField` directly — body is a JSON string; do `JSON.parse(r.body).someField`
- ❌ Hardcoding menu data in `app.js` as JS arrays — always put content in `dataRecords`
- ❌ Wrapping init logic in a bare async IIFE with no error handler — errors are silently swallowed;
   always end with `.catch(err => document.getElementById('app').innerHTML = err.message)`
- ❌ Calling `uix.state.insert()` without `uix.state.find()` on startup — state is **never** auto-injected;
   always restore it in `init()`: `const saved = await uix.state.find({ type: '...' }); cart = saved.map(r => JSON.parse(r.body));`
- ❌ Passing extra fields directly to `uix.state.insert()` instead of using `body` — always use `{ type, body: { ...yourData } }`,
   otherwise `item.body` will be null when you read it back

---

## Packages and tools

| Package | Install | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `@dotuix/core` | `npm install @dotuix/core` | Core library: pack, unpack, validate, sign, read SQLite |
| `@dotuix/cli` | `npm install -g @dotuix/cli` | CLI: dotuix pack / validate / init / sign / encrypt / export |
| `@dotuix/mcp` | `npm install @dotuix/mcp` | MCP server: lets AI agents generate and pack .uix files via tool calls |
| `@dotuix/ai` | `npm install @dotuix/ai` | One-function SDK for AI-generated code: `createUIX({ manifest, files })` → `.uix` path |
| `@dotuix/vite-plugin` | `npm install @dotuix/vite-plugin` | Vite plugin: build React/Vue/Svelte/TS apps → .uix |
| VS Code extension | `intenttext.dotuix` | Manifest IntelliSense, .uix file icon, pack/validate commands |

## Links

- Website: https://dotuix.uts.qa
- GitHub: https://github.com/dotuix/dotuix
- npm (@dotuix/core): https://www.npmjs.com/package/@dotuix/core
- npm (@dotuix/cli): https://www.npmjs.com/package/@dotuix/cli
- VS Code Extension: https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=intenttext.dotuix
- Format spec (this file): https://dotuix.uts.qa/llms.txt
